name: AFFIRM fisetin older-women frailty trial is enrolling with no posted results
summary: AFFIRM is direct fisetin functional trial-watch evidence for Senolytics, but it cannot support efficacy because results are not posted.
statement: ClinicalTrials.gov lists NCT03430037 as an enrolling-by-invitation phase 2 randomized double-blind fisetin trial in older women with estimated enrollment of 40, improved 6-minute walk as the primary endpoint, estimated primary completion in November 2026, estimated study completion in November 2027, and no posted results in the June 11, 2026 API view.
population_or_model: Older women in a fisetin frailty and inflammation trial
time_horizon: Estimated primary completion November 2026 and study completion November 2027
quantitative_note: Estimated enrollment is 40; randomized allocation; double masking; primary outcome is improved 6-minute walk.
caveats: No-results registry status is not evidence of benefit or harm.
Functional primary endpoint data are not yet available.
Results should be interpreted alongside the companion older-adults AFFIRM-LITE inflammation-marker trial.
